items.text:
Hi Jordan,

A package arrived for you and is being held at the Lincoln Hall mailroom. Please pick it up by Friday, May 9. Bring your student ID. A $5 storage fee applies after that date.

Best,
Maya, Resident Assistant

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Jordan bring to the mailroom?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Jordan to bring a student ID when picking up the package.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about picking up the package on May 10?
questions.explanation: Since a $5 storage fee applies after May 9, picking up on May 10 would mean a charge is added.

items.text: NOTICE: Fire alarms in Bradley Residence Hall will be tested on Tuesday, October 14, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM. Residents are advised that alarms will sound intermittently. No evacuation is required during this scheduled testing. Direct questions to the Facilities Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the testing take place?
questions.explanation: The notice states testing occurs on Tuesday, October 14, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM, which is morning.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should residents do during the testing?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies that no evacuation is required, meaning residents should remain in the building.

items.text: The Rolling Bites truck is parked by the Student Union today until 3 PM, and the tacos are amazing! They're running a special: any taco for just $3. Cash or card accepted. Don't miss it before they roll out!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is being offered for $3?
questions.explanation: The post states the special is any taco for just $3.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about visiting the truck at 4 PM today?
questions.explanation: Since the truck is parked only until 3 PM, it would have left by 4 PM.

items.text: Heads up, everyone! The Tech Commons lab is offering free printing through Friday, May 16. After that, the usual $0.10 per page returns. Great chance to print those final project handouts. Just swipe your ID at the front desk to start!

questions.stem: According to the post, what must students do to begin printing?
questions.explanation: The post says to swipe your ID at the front desk to start printing.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about printing on Saturday, May 17?
questions.explanation: Since free printing ends Friday, May 16, and the $0.10 per page rate returns, printing on May 17 would cost money.

items.text: NOTICE: The Hartwell Library study rooms may now be reserved online. Reservations are limited to two hours per group and must be made at least one day in advance. Rooms not claimed within fifteen minutes will be released. Visit the circulation desk for assistance.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how far in advance must reservations be made?
questions.explanation: The notice states reservations must be made at least one day in advance.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a group arriving twenty minutes late?
questions.explanation: Since rooms not claimed within fifteen minutes are released, a group arriving twenty minutes late would lose their room.

items.text:
Dear Sam,

The writing tutor you requested is available this Wednesday, April 9, at 2:00 PM in Room 215. Sessions last fifty minutes. Please reply to confirm, and bring a draft of your essay. We look forward to seeing you.

Regards,
The Writing Center

questions.stem: According to the email, what is Sam asked to bring?
questions.explanation: The email asks Sam to bring a draft of the essay to the session.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the session if Sam does not reply?
questions.explanation: Since the email asks Sam to reply to confirm, the session might not be held without confirmation.

items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! This Saturday, March 8, from 9 AM to 1 PM in Marshall Hall, Room 100. Hundreds of titles priced from $5. Cash only. Sponsored by the Student Council. Arrive early for the best selection. All proceeds support campus clubs!

questions.stem: According to the advertisement, how may buyers pay?
questions.explanation: The advertisement states the sale is cash only.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about arriving when the sale opens?
questions.explanation: The ad advises arriving early for the best selection, so arriving at opening would mean a better selection.

items.text: NOTICE: South Lot C will be closed for repaving on Monday, June 2, from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Vehicles must be removed by 6:00 AM that day. Permit holders may park in North Lot A at no charge. Contact Campus Parking with questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, by when must vehicles be removed?
questions.explanation: The notice states vehicles must be removed by 6:00 AM on June 2.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about permit holders using North Lot A that day?
questions.explanation: The notice states permit holders may park in North Lot A at no charge, meaning they would park free.

items.text:
Notice: Temporary Closure of the West Wing Study Commons

Date: September 8
Location: Harmon Library, Third Floor

The West Wing Study Commons will be closed from Monday, September 15, through Friday, September 19, for the installation of new ventilation equipment. During this period, all 24-hour study access will be relocated to the East Reading Room on the second floor.

Students who reserved group rooms in the West Wing should note the following:

- Existing reservations will be automatically transferred to comparable rooms in the East Reading Room.
- A confirmation email will be sent to your campus address by September 12.
- Lockers in the West Wing must be emptied before 6:00 PM on September 12. Items left behind will be moved to the lost-and-found desk.
- A replacement key for any lost locker key may be obtained for a $5 fee at the circulation desk.

The West Wing is expected to reopen on Saturday, September 20, with extended weekend hours. Quiet study is still available throughout the building during construction, though some noise may occur between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM.

For questions, please contact the library help desk at help@harmonlibrary.edu or visit the front office.

format_metadata.title: Notice: Temporary Closure of the West Wing Study Commons

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do before 6:00 PM on September 12?

options.A: Confirm their group room reservations by email
options.B: Remove their belongings from the West Wing lockers
options.C: Pay a $5 fee at the circulation desk
options.D: Relocate to the East Reading Room study area

questions.explanation: The notice states that lockers in the West Wing must be emptied before 6:00 PM on September 12. Option A swaps the September 12 date with the confirmation email (which is sent, not done by students). Option C uses the real $5 fee but applies it to a different, optional situation (a lost key). Option D borrows the East Reading Room but attaches it to the wrong deadline.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about study options during the closure?

options.A: All study spaces will be completely unavailable during the week
options.B: Group rooms will no longer be offered anywhere on campus
options.C: The East Reading Room will charge a fee for overnight access
options.D: Students can still find quiet places to study in the building

questions.explanation: The notice says quiet study is still available throughout the building during construction, implying students can continue to study there. Option A reverses this logic with the absolute 'completely unavailable.' Option B contradicts the transfer of reservations to comparable rooms. Option C invents a fee for overnight access that the text never mentions.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform students about a temporary closure and related arrangements
options.B: To announce permanent new weekend hours for the entire library
options.C: To advertise the installation of upgraded ventilation equipment
options.D: To remind students about the cost of replacing lost locker keys

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to inform students of the West Wing's temporary closure and the resulting changes to access, reservations, and lockers. Option B exaggerates with 'permanent' and 'entire library.' Option C captures only one detail (the equipment). Option D is too narrow, focusing on a single minor point about locker key fees.

items.text:
Subject: New Procedure for Reserving Lab Equipment

Dear Engineering Students,

Starting October 1, all reservations for equipment in the Fabrication Lab must be submitted through the online maintenance portal rather than the paper sign-up sheet. We're making this change so that equipment can be inspected and serviced between bookings, which should reduce the breakdowns many of you reported last semester.

To reserve a machine, log in to the portal with your student ID, select an available time slot, and confirm your booking at least 24 hours in advance. A refundable $10 deposit is required for the laser cutter and the 3D printers; the deposit is returned automatically once staff confirm the equipment was left clean and undamaged.

Please note that walk-in use will no longer be permitted after October 1. If you cannot make a reserved slot, cancel it online so another student can claim the time. Repeated no-shows may result in a one-week suspension of your booking privileges.

If you have trouble accessing the portal, stop by Room 112 in Whitmore Hall during staff hours, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

Best regards,
Daniel Okafor
Lab Operations Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: New Procedure for Reserving Lab Equipment

questions.stem: According to the email, what is required to reserve the laser cutter?

options.A: A signature on the paper sign-up sheet in the lab
options.B: A refundable deposit submitted along with the booking
options.C: A staff inspection completed before each time slot
options.D: A written request delivered to Room 112 in person

questions.explanation: The email states a refundable $10 deposit is required for the laser cutter and 3D printers. Option A refers to the old paper sheet that is being discontinued. Option C uses real words ('staff', 'inspection') but the inspection happens between bookings, not as a reservation requirement. Option D borrows Room 112 but applies it to a fabricated written-request process.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who repeatedly miss their reserved slots?

options.A: They will receive a full refund of their equipment deposit
options.B: They must switch back to using the paper sign-up sheet
options.C: They may temporarily lose the ability to book equipment
options.D: They will be charged an additional fee for each absence

questions.explanation: The email warns that repeated no-shows may result in a one-week suspension of booking privileges, implying a temporary loss of access. Option A reverses the consequence into a reward. Option B introduces the discontinued paper sheet as a penalty, which is not supported. Option D invents an extra fee that the text never mentions.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind students of the staff hours in Whitmore Hall
options.B: To explain why equipment broke down during last semester
options.C: To announce a new online system for reserving lab equipment
options.D: To request that students clean machines after every booking

questions.explanation: The email's overall purpose is to announce the shift to an online portal for reserving lab equipment and explain how it works. Option A captures only a minor closing detail. Option B addresses one reason for the change, not the main point. Option D focuses on a single condition tied to the deposit rather than the email's purpose.

items.text:
Notice: Updated Policy for the Campus Shuttle Service

Date: November 3
Departure Point: Carver Student Center, North Entrance

Beginning November 10, the campus shuttle will operate on a revised schedule to better serve evening classes. Shuttles will run every 20 minutes from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM on weekdays and every 40 minutes on weekends. The late-night route to the residence halls has been extended to midnight.

All riders are reminded of the following policies:

- A valid student ID must be shown to the driver upon boarding.
- A semester shuttle pass may be purchased for $45 at the transit office in Carver Student Center.
- Single rides are free for students but cost $2.00 for guests and visitors.
- Bicycles are permitted only on the exterior rack and must be secured by the rider.

The transit office encourages students to download the TrackMyRide app, through which real-time shuttle locations can be viewed. Lost items are held at the transit office for fourteen days before being donated.

Riders with accessibility needs should request a lift-equipped shuttle at least one day in advance by calling the transit office at 555-0148.

format_metadata.title: Notice: Updated Policy for the Campus Shuttle Service

questions.stem: According to the notice, how often will shuttles run on weekdays?

options.A: Every 40 minutes throughout the entire day
options.B: Every 20 minutes between morning and night
options.C: Every 14 minutes during peak class times
options.D: Every hour after the late-night route ends

questions.explanation: The notice states shuttles will run every 20 minutes from 7:00 AM to 11:00 PM on weekdays. Option A uses the real 40-minute interval but misapplies it (that is the weekend frequency). Option C borrows the number 14 from the lost-items policy and attaches it to the wrong context. Option D fabricates an hourly schedule not mentioned in the text.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a visitor who wants to ride the shuttle?

options.A: They must purchase a semester pass before boarding
options.B: They are not allowed to ride the campus shuttle at all
options.C: They can request a lift-equipped shuttle without notice
options.D: They will pay a fare that students are not charged

questions.explanation: Single rides are free for students but cost $2.00 for guests and visitors, implying visitors pay a fare students avoid. Option A wrongly requires the $45 semester pass, which is optional. Option B contradicts the policy by denying visitors access entirely. Option C ignores the rule that lift-equipped shuttles require one day's advance notice.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a revised shuttle schedule and rider policies
options.B: To promote the download of the TrackMyRide tracking app
options.C: To explain how lost items are stored at the transit office
options.D: To describe the cost of a semester pass for the shuttle

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to inform riders of the new shuttle schedule and the policies that govern its use. Option B captures only one supporting detail (the app). Option C is too narrow, addressing just the lost-items rule. Option D focuses on a single price rather than the full set of changes.

items.text:
Notice: Fall Sustainability Fair on the Quad

Date: October 5
Location: Main Quad, in front of Bradley Hall

The Office of Campus Life invites all students to attend the annual Sustainability Fair on Saturday, October 18, from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM. The event will be held on the Main Quad and will move indoors to the Bradley Hall atrium in case of rain.

This year's fair will feature local vendors, student organizations, and hands-on workshops on composting and energy conservation. Attendees can look forward to the following:

- A free reusable water bottle for the first 200 visitors.
- A clothing swap where students may exchange gently used items at no cost.
- A repair station where volunteers will fix small electronics and bicycles.
- A raffle for a $50 campus bookstore gift card; tickets are $1.00 each.

Students who wish to volunteer at the fair should sign up through the Campus Life portal by October 12. Volunteers will receive a free lunch and a certificate of participation.

Proceeds from the raffle support the campus community garden. For more information, please email campuslife@bradleyu.edu.

format_metadata.title: Notice: Fall Sustainability Fair on the Quad
format_metadata.location: Main Quad, in front of Bradley Hall

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will the first 200 visitors receive?

options.A: A ticket for the campus bookstore raffle
options.B: A free reusable water bottle at the fair
options.C: A certificate for completing a workshop
options.D: A gently used item from the clothing swap

questions.explanation: The notice states the first 200 visitors will receive a free reusable water bottle. Option A refers to the raffle, whose tickets cost $1.00 and are not tied to the first 200 visitors. Option C borrows 'certificate' from the volunteer benefits and misapplies it. Option D uses the clothing swap but wrongly frames it as a giveaway for early arrivals.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to volunteer at the fair?

options.A: They must pay a fee to receive their free lunch
options.B: They are required to bring their own repair tools
options.C: They cannot attend the fair as regular visitors
options.D: They need to register before the event takes place

questions.explanation: The notice says volunteers should sign up through the portal by October 12, before the October 18 fair, implying advance registration is needed. Option A contradicts the stated free lunch by adding a fee. Option B invents a tool requirement not mentioned. Option C wrongly assumes volunteering excludes someone from attending.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a campus fair and its featured activities
options.B: To raise funds specifically for the community garden
options.C: To recruit volunteers for the Office of Campus Life
options.D: To explain the rules for entering the bookstore raffle

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to invite students to the Sustainability Fair and describe what it offers. Option B captures only the raffle's beneficiary, a single detail. Option C focuses narrowly on the volunteer sign-up. Option D addresses just the raffle rules rather than the broader event.
